mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) like Kimi-K2.5 and GLM-5. It provides a simple interface to run and manage models, making it suitable for developers looking to integrate AI capabilities into their applications. Key trade-offs include ease of use versus the need for underlying infrastructure management.

steps.title: Install the Ollama CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Ollama platform.

```bash

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

irm https://ollama.com/install.ps1 | iex

```

> Use this command for Linux and macOS.
> Use this command for Windows.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create ollama-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and configured.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to interact with your cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy Ollama on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys the Ollama application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ollama/ollama/main/deploy/ollama-deployment.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains the deployment configuration for Ollama.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for the Ollama deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment ollama --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ollama/ollama/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This sets up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> This YAML file sets up monitoring for the Ollama deployment.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ollama deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss for any models stored in the Ollama instance.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ollama/ollama/main/deploy/ollama-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete ollama-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This will also delete the cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important models. This step will upgrade the Ollama deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -l app=ollama -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ollama/ollama/main/deploy/ollama-deployment.yaml

kubectl get pods

```

> Drain the pods to prepare for upgrade.
> Reapply the deployment to upgrade.
> Verify that the new pods are running.
commands: kubectl get pods -l app=ollama -o name | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama image may not be available or the image name is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name in the deployment YAML and ensure it is correct. You can also try pulling the image manually.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Ollama application may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'FailedScheduling'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may not be enough resources available in the cluster to schedule the Ollama pods.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in the deployment YAML and adjust your cluster size if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account may not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account used by the Ollama deployment has the correct roles assigned.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models including Kimi-K2.5 and GLM-5, improved API response times.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old model loading method, which may affect existing integrations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your model loading scripts to use the new API endpoints.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of running pods with the Ollama application in the 'Running' state when you execute 'kubectl get pods'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster set up and the necessary tools installed.
